name: Billy Connolly
bio: Sir Billy Connolly is a Scottish comedian, actor and musician from Glasgow who began as a folk singer before turning to stand-up in the early 1970s. Known for long, profane, improvised storytelling rooted in working-class Scottish life, he has topped numerous polls as the greatest stand-up of all time. He retired from live performance in the 2010s following a Parkinson's diagnosis.

timeline.ev: Theatrical debut with the revue Connollys Glasgow Flourish
Solo Concert album and Parkinson appearance bring national fame
World Tour of Scotland TV travelogue series
Diagnosed with Parkinsons disease; later steps back from stand-up
Knighted at Buckingham Palace for services to entertainment and charity
Received the BAFTA Fellowship for lifetime achievement

accolades: Knighted for services to entertainment and charity (2017)
Frequently voted greatest stand-up comedian of all time in UK polls
